body.u: As I look out of my window and see my grandchildren playing in the big playing field, I wonder if their life would be like mine. I doubt it very much. Things seem to change over the years I never grew up like my mother. She was so different from me.

When I left school I lived with my stepmother, my real mother left all of us children and my father when I was twelve then my father when I was twelve then my father died two years later. So I was left with a step-mother. When I left school I had no qualifications although I went in for quite a lot. I failed everything, but I got a good grade in art. Which wasn’t much use. I went straight on the dole for a couple of months until my brother got me a job with him in a garage. I wasn’t any mechanic just a petrol pump attendant. I stayed there for a few years because I liked the job. I was quite contented to get a job and to get on well with the people I worked with. It wasn’t boring because I met all different kinds of people.
While I had this job I got engaged to the boy who I was going out with while I was still at school. Just as I started on another job which was working with my cousin in a fashion shop I got married.

We had a beautiful wedding. Well I thought it was beautiful but to any other person it could have been just another wedding to them. My eldest brother gave me away and my youngest sister was a bridesmaid because when she was living at home with us she decided to go and live with her real mother. She was only ten at the time. My real mother came to the wedding as well but I wouldn’t invite the man she had ran off with, I always have hated him and I always will. After the wedding we went on honeymoon to Tenariff for a week, it was lovely.
After the honey-moon I went back to the job in the shop and Larry went back to his job in the dockyard.

Two years later we had a baby boy. We named him Paul after my brother and Larry’s cousin. I had finished work just before I had Paul, to become a housewife. At first life was exciting to have a baby to look after and a husband a house but as the years went by it became dreadfully boring, until I had another child. This time a girl. We named her Sam, short for Samantha. When Sam was three we moved away from Sheerness to London. We didntdidn't like it in London much so we moved again to Rochester. Here we settled. Larry got a job and with the help of a friend gradually built up a small business. When both of the children went to school I started on another job in a factory but it was terrible and I packed it up very quickly.
Later I had another boy. We named him Mark. After Mark I didntdidn't get another job I was quite content to be a mother, wife and housewife.
Now my childrenschildren's children have grown, I wonder what their future will be.
I hope it will be as good or better than mine. Everyone had their ups and downs. LetsLet's just hope there wontwon't be many downs.
